monsters.name: Fire Elemental
monsters.description:
Fire Elemental

Fire Aura: At the end of each of the fire elemental's turns, each creature of the fire elemental's choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the fire elemental takes damage. Creatures and flammable objects in the Emanation start burning.

Fire Form: The fire elemental can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so, and it can enter a creature's space and stop there. The first time it enters a creature's space on a turn, that creature takes damage.

Illumination: The fire elemental sheds Bright Light in a 30-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 30 feet.

Water Susceptibility: The fire elemental takes 3 (1d6) Cold damage damage for every 5 feet the fire elemental moves in water or for every gallon of water splashed on it.

Multiattack: The fire elemental makes two Burn attacks.

Action — Burn (2d6 fire): If the target is a creature or a flammable object, it starts burning.

At Will: Animal Friendship, Charm Monster (lasts 24 hours; ends early if the dryad casts the spell again), Druidcraft
1/Day Each: Entangle, Pass without Trace

Tree Stride: If within 5 feet of a Large or bigger tree, the dryad teleports to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of a second that is within 60 feet of the previous tree.

monsters.name: Goblin Boss
monsters.description:
Goblins
Goblin Boss

Action — Scimitar (1d6 slashing): plus 1d4 slashing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Action — Shortbow (1d6 piercing): plus 1d4 piercing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Multiattack: The goblin boss makes two attacks, using Scimitar or Shortbow in any combination.

Nimble Escape: The goblin boss takes the Disengage or Hide action.

Redirect Attack: Trigger: A creature the goblin boss can see makes an attack roll against it.
Response: The goblin boss chooses a Small or Medium ally within 5 feet of itself. The goblin boss and that ally swap places, and the ally becomes the target of the attack instead.

monsters.name: Goblin Minion
monsters.description:
Goblins
Goblin Minion

Action — Dagger (1d4 piercing)

Nimble Escape: The goblin minion takes the Disengage or Hide action.

monsters.name: Goblin Warrior
monsters.description:
Goblins
Goblin Warrior

Action — Scimitar (1d6 slashing): plus 1d4 slashing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Action — Shortbow (1d6 piercing): plus 1d4 piercing damage if the attack roll had Advantage.

Nimble Escape: The goblin warrior takes the Disengage or Hide action.

monsters.name: Green Hag
monsters.description:
Amphibious: The green hag can breathe air and water.

Coven Magic: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the green hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The green hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Mimicry: The green hag can mimic animal sounds and humanoid voices. A creature that hears the sounds can tell they are imitations only with a successful .

Multiattack: The green hag makes two Claw attacks.

Action — Claw (1d8 slashing)

Spellcasting: The green hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC , +4 to hit with spell attacks):
At Will: Dancing Lights, Disguise Self (24-hour duration), Invisibility (self only, and the hag leaves no tracks while Invisible), Minor Illusion, Ray of Sickness (level 3 version)

monsters.name: Hobgoblin Captain
monsters.description:
Hobgoblins
Hobgoblin Captain

Action — Greatsword (2d6 slashing)

Action — Longbow (1d8 piercing)

Aura of Authority: While in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the hobgoblin captain, the hobgoblin captain and its allies have Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws, provided the hobgoblin captain doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Multiattack: The hobgoblin captain makes two attacks, using Greatsword or Longbow in any combination.

monsters.name: Hobgoblin Warrior
monsters.description:
Hobgoblins
Hobgoblin Warrior

Action — Longsword (2d10 slashing)

Action — Longbow (1d8 piercing)

Pack Tactics: The hobgoblin warrior has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hobgoblin warrior's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

monsters.name: Satyr
monsters.description:
Magic Resistance: The satyr has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Action — Hooves (1d4 bludgeoning): If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, the satyr pushes the target up to 10 feet straight away from itself.

Mockery: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , the satyr can see within 90 feet.
Failure: damage.

monsters.name: Sea Hag
monsters.description:
Amphibious: The sea hag can breathe air and water.

Coven Magic: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the sea hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The sea hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Vile Appearance: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , any Beast or Humanoid that starts its turn within 30 feet of the sea hag and can see the sea hag's true form.
Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn.
Success: The target is immune to this sea hag's Horrific Appearance for 24 hours.

Action — Claw (2d6 slashing)

Death Glare: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , one Frightened creature the sea hag can see within 30 feet.
Failure: If the target has 20 Hit Points or fewer, it drops to 0 Hit Points. Otherwise, the target takes damage.

Illusory Appearance: The sea hag casts Disguise Self, using Constitution as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ). The spell's duration is 24 hours.

monsters.name: Sprite
monsters.description:
Sprite

Action — Needle Sword (1d4 piercing)

Action — Enchanting Bow: and the target has the Charmed condition until the start of the sprite's next turn.

Heart Sight: Charisma Saving Throw: DC , one creature within 5 feet the sprite can see (Celestials, Fiends, and Undead automatically fail the save).
Failure: The sprite knows the target's emotions and alignment.

Invisibility: The sprite casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

monsters.name: Worg
monsters.description:
Worgs
Worg

Action — Bite (1d8 piercing): and the next attack roll made against the target before the start of the worg's next turn has Advantage.

monsters.name: Balor
monsters.description:
Balor

Death Throes: The balor explodes when it dies.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the balor.
Failure: damage.
Failure or Success: If the balor dies outside the Abyss, it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Fire Aura: At the end of each of the balor's turns, each creature of the balor's choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the balor takes damage.

Legendary Resistance: If the balor fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance: The balor has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The balor makes one Flame Whip attack and one Lightning Blade attack.

Action — Flame Whip (3d6 force): If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, the balor pulls the target up to 25 feet straight toward itself, and the target has the Prone condition.

Teleport: The balor teleports itself or a willing demon within 10 feet of itself up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space the balor can see.

Action — Lightning Blade (3d8 force): and the target can't take Reaction until the start of the balor's next turn.

monsters.name: Barbed Devil
monsters.description:
Barbed Devil

Barbed Hide: At the start of each of its turns, the barbed devil deals damage to any creature it is grappling or any creature grappling it.

Diabolical Restoration: If the barbed devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The barbed devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The barbed devil makes one Claws attack and one Tail attack, or it makes two Hurl Flame attacks.

Action — Claws (2d6 slashing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from both claws.

Action — Tail (2d10 bludgeoning)

Action — Hurl Flame: If the target is a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it starts burning.

monsters.name: Bearded Devil
monsters.description:
Bearded Devil

Magic Resistance: The bearded devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The bearded devil makes one Beard attack and one Infernal Glaive attack.

Action — Beard (1d8 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the bearded devil's next turn. Until this poison ends, the target can't regain Hit Points.

Action — Infernal Glaive (1d10 slashing): If the target is a creature and doesn't already have an infernal wound, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target receives an infernal wound. While wounded, the target loses Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. The wound closes after 1 minute, after a spell restores Hit Points to the target, or after the target or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to stanch the wound, doing so by succeeding on a .

monsters.name: Bone Devil
monsters.description:
Bone Devil

Diabolical Restoration: If the bone devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The bone devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The bone devil makes two Claw attacks and one Infernal Sting attack.

Action — Claw (2d8 slashing)

Action — Infernal Sting (2d10 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the bone devil's next turn. While Poisoned, the target can't regain Hit Points.

monsters.name: Chain Devil
monsters.description:
Chain Devil

Diabolical Restoration: If the chain devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The chain devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The chain devil makes two Chain attacks and uses Conjure Infernal Chain.

Conjure Infernal Chain: The devil conjures a fiery chain to bind a creature.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , the chain devil can see within 60 feet.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Restrained condition until the end of the chain devil's next turn, at which point the chain disappears. If the target is Large or smaller, the devil moves the target up to 30 feet straight toward itself.
Success: The chain disappears.

Unnerving Gaze: Trigger: A creature the chain devil can see starts its turn within 30 feet of the chain devil and can see the chain devil.
Response—Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , the triggering creature.
Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the end of its turn.
Success: The target is immune to this chain devil's Unnerving Gaze for 24 hours.

Action — Chain (2d6 slashing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from one of two chains, and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

monsters.name: Dretch
monsters.description:
Dretches
Dretch

Action — Rend (1d6 slashing)

Fetid Cloud: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the dretch.
Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn. While Poisoned, the creature can take either an action or a Bonus Action on its turn, not both, and it can't take Reactions.

monsters.name: Erinyes
monsters.description:
Erinyes

Diabolical Restoration: If the erinyes dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The erinyes has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Magic Rope: The erinyes has a magic rope. While bearing it, the erinyes can use the Entangling Rope action. The rope has AC 20, HP 90, and Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage. The rope turns to dust if reduced to 0 Hit Points, if it is 5+ feet away from the erinyes for 1 hour or more, or if the erinyes dies. If the rope is damaged or destroyed, the erinyes can fully restore it when finishing a Short or Long Rest.

Multiattack: The erinyes makes three Withering Sword attacks and can use Entangling Rope.

Parry: Trigger: The erinyes is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The erinyes adds 4 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

Action — Withering Sword (2d8 slashing)

Entangling Rope: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one creature the erinyes can see within 120 feet.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Restrained condition until the rope is destroyed, the erinyes uses a Bonus Action to release the target, or the erinyes uses Entangling Rope again.

monsters.name: Glabrezu
monsters.description:
Glabrezu

Demonic Restoration: If the glabrezu dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Magic Resistance: The glabrezu has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The glabrezu makes two Pincer attacks and uses Pummel or Spellcasting.

Action — Pincer (2d10 slashing): If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from one of two pincers.

Pummel: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one creature Grappled by the glabrezu.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting: The glabrezu casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Darkness, Detect Magic, Dispel Magic
1/Day Each: Confusion, Fly, Power Word Stun

monsters.name: Gnoll Warrior
monsters.description:
Gnolls
Gnoll Warrior

Action — Rend (1d6 piercing)

Action — Bone Bow (1d10 piercing)

monsters.name: Hell Hound
monsters.description:
Hell Hound

Pack Tactics: The hell hound has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the hell hound's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Multiattack: The hell hound makes two Bite attacks.

Action — Bite (1d8 piercing)

Fire Breath: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Cone.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

monsters.name: Hezrou
monsters.description:
Hezrou

Demonic Restoration: If the hezrou dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Magic Resistance: The hezrou has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Stench: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , any creature (other than a hezrou) that starts its turn in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the hezrou.
Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition until the start of its next turn.
Success: The target is immune to the Stench of all hezrou for 1 hour.

Multiattack: The hezrou makes three Rend attacks.

Action — Rend (1d4 slashing)

Leap: The hezrou jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.

monsters.name: Horned Devil
monsters.description:
Horned Devil

Diabolical Restoration: If the horned devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The horned devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The horned devil makes three attacks, using Searing Fork or Hurl Flame in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Infernal Tail.

Action — Searing Fork (2d8 piercing)

Action — Hurl Flame (5d8 fire): If the target is a flammable object that isn't being worn or carried, it starts burning.

Infernal Tail: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one creature the horned devil can see within 10 ft.
Failure: damage, and the target receives an infernal wound if it doesn't have one. While wounded, the target loses Hit Points at the start of each of its turns. The wound closes after 1 minute, after a spell restores Hit Points to the target, or after the target or a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to stanch the wound, doing so by succeeding on a DC 17 Wisdom (Medicine).

monsters.name: Ice Devil
monsters.description:
Ice Devil

Diabolical Restoration: If the ice devil dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Magic Resistance: The ice devil has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The ice devil makes three Ice Spear attacks. It can replace one attack with a Tail attack.

Action — Tail (3d6 bludgeoning)

Ice Wall: The ice devil casts Wall of Ice (level 8 version), requiring no spell components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ).

Action — Ice Spear (2d8 piercing): Hit: damage. Until the end of its next turn, the target can't take a Bonus Action or Reaction, its Speed decreases by 10 feet, and it can move or take one action on its turn, not both.
Hit or Miss: The spear magically returns to the ice devil's hand immediately after a ranged attack.

monsters.name: Imp
monsters.description:
Imp

Magic Resistance: The imp has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Action — Sting (1d6 piercing)

Invisibility: The imp casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

Shape-Shift: The imp shape-shifts to resemble a rat (Speed 20 ft.), a raven (20 ft, Fly 60 ft.), or a spider (20 ft, Climb 20 ft.), or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics are the same in each form, except for its Speed. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monsters.name: Incubus
monsters.description:
Incubus

Succubus Form: When the incubus finishes a Long Rest, it can shape-shift into a Succubus, using that stat block instead of this one. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Action — Restless Touch (3d6 psychic): and the target is cursed for 24 hours or until the incubus dies. Until the curse ends, the target gains no benefit from finishing Short Rests.

Multiattack: The incubus makes two Restless Touch attacks.

Spellcasting: The incubus casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Disguise Self, Etherealness
1/Day Each: Dream, Hypnotic Pattern

Nightmare: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , one creature the incubus can see within 60 feet.
Failure: If the target has 20 Hit Points or fewer, it has the Unconscious condition for 1 hour, until it takes damage, or until a creature within 5 feet of it takes an action to wake it. Otherwise, the target takes damage.

monsters.name: Lamia
monsters.description:
Lamia

Multiattack: The lamia makes two Claw attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Corrupting Touch.

Action — Claw (1d8 slashing)

Action — Corrupting Touch: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , the lamia can see within 5 feet.
Failure: damage, and the target is cursed for 1 hour. Until the curse ends, the target has the Charmed and Poisoned conditions.

Spellcasting: The lamia casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Disguise Self (can appear as a Large or Medium biped), Minor Illusion
1/Day Each: Geas, Major Image, Scrying

Leap: The lamia jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.

monsters.name: Lemure
monsters.description:
Lemures
Lemure

Hellish Restoration: If the lemure dies in the Nine Hells, it revives with all its Hit Points in 1d10 days unless it is killed by a creature under the effects of a Bless spell or its remains are sprinkled with Holy Water.

Action — Vile Slime (1d4 poison)

monsters.name: Marilith
monsters.description:
Marilith

Magic Resistance: The marilith has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Demonic Restoration: If the marilith dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Reactive: The marilith can take one Reaction on every turn of combat.

Multiattack: The marilith makes six Pact Blade attacks and uses Constrict.

Action — Pact Blade (1d10 slashing)

Constrict: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature the marilith can see within 5 feet.
Failure: damage. The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC ), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Teleport: The marilith teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

Parry: Trigger: The marilith is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The marilith adds 5 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

monsters.name: Nalfeshnee
monsters.description:
Nalfeshnee

Demonic Restoration: If the nalfeshnee dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Magic Resistance: The nalfeshnee has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The nalfeshnee makes three Rend attacks.

Teleport: The nalfeshnee teleports up to 120 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.

Action — Rend (2d10 slashing)

Horror Nimbus: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Emanation originating from the nalfeshnee.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Frightened condition for 1 minute, until it takes damage, or until it ends its turn with the nalfeshnee out of line of sight.
Success: The target is immune to this nalfeshnee's Horror Nimbus for 24 hours.

Pursuit: Trigger: Another creature the nalfeshnee can see ends its move within 120 feet of the nalfeshnee. Response: The nalfeshnee uses Teleport, but its destination space must be within 10 feet of the triggering creature.

monsters.name: Night Hag
monsters.description:
Night Hag

Coven Magic: While within 30 feet of at least two hag allies, the night hag can cast one of the following spells, requiring no Material components, using the spell's normal casting time, and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ): Augury, Find Familiar, Identify, Locate Object, Scrying, or Unseen Servant. The night hag must finish a Long Rest before using this trait to cast that spell again.

Magic Resistance: The night hag has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Soul Bag: The night hag has a soul bag. While holding or carrying the bag, the night hag can use its Nightmare Haunting action.
The bag has AC 15, HP 20, and Resistance to all damage. The bag turns to dust if reduced to 0 Hit Points. If the bag is destroyed, any souls the bag is holding are released. The night hag can create a new bag after 7 days.

Multiattack: The night hag makes two Claw attacks.

Action — Claw (2d8 slashing)

Spellcasting: The night hag casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Etherealness, Magic Missile (level 4 version)
2/Day Each: Phantasmal Killer, Plane Shift (self only)

Shape-Shift: The night hag shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Other than its size, its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Nightmare Haunting: While on the Ethereal Plane, the night hag casts Dream, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting. Only the night hag can serve as the spell's messenger, and the target must be a creature the night hag can see on the Material Plane. The spell fails and is wasted if the target is under the effect of the Protection from Evil and Good spell or within a Magic Circle spell.
If the target takes damage from the Dream spell, the target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to that damage. If the spell kills the target, its soul is trapped in the night hag's soul bag, and the target can't be raised from the dead until its soul is released.

monsters.name: Nightmare
monsters.description:
Nightmare

Confer Fire Resistance: The nightmare can grant Resistance to Fire damage to a rider while it is on the nightmare.

Illumination: The nightmare sheds Bright Light in a 10-foot radius and Dim Light for an additional 10 feet.

Action — Hooves (2d8 bludgeoning)

Ethereal Stride: The nightmare and up to within 5 ft. of it teleport to the Ethereal Plane from the Material Plane or vice versa.
monsters.languages: Understands Abyssal, Common, and Infernal but can’t speak

monsters.name: Oni
monsters.description:
Oni

Regeneration: The oni regains Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Multiattack: The oni makes two Claw or Nightmare Ray attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting.

Action — Claw (1d12 slashing)

Action — Nightmare Ray (2d6 psychic): and the target has the Frightened condition until the start of the oni's next turn.

Shape-Shift: The oni shape-shifts into a Small or Medium Humanoid or a Large Giant, or it returns to its true form. Other than its size, its game statistics are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Spellcasting: The oni casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
1/Day Each: Charm Person (level 2 version), Darkness, Gaseous Form, Sleep

Invisibility: The oni casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

monsters.name: Pit Fiend
monsters.description:
Pit Fiend

Diabolical Restoration: If the pit fiend dies outside the Nine Hells, its body disappears in sulfurous smoke, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Nine Hells.

Fear Aura: The pit fiend emanates an aura in a 20-foot Emanation while it doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.
Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , any enemy that starts its turn in the aura.
Failure: The target has the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn.
Success: The target is immune to this pit fiend's aura for 24 hours.

Magic Resistance: The pit fiend has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The pit fiend makes one Bite attack, two Devilish Claw attacks, and one Fiery Mace attack.

Legendary Resistance: If the pit fiend fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Action — Bite (3d6 piercing): If the target is a creature, it must make the following saving throw.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition. While Poisoned, the target can't regain Hit Points and takes damage at the start of each of its turns, and it repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

Action — Devilish Claw (4d8 necrotic)

Action — Fiery Mace (4d6 force)

Hellfire Spellcasting: The pit fiend casts Fireball (level 5 version) twice, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ).. It can replace one Fireball with Hold Monster (level 7 version) or Wall of Fire.

monsters.name: Quasit
monsters.description:
Quasit

Magic Resistance: The quasit has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Action — Rend (1d4 slashing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the quasit's next turn.

Invisibility: The quasit casts Invisibility on itself, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability.

Shape-Shift: The quasit shape-shifts to resemble a bat (Speed 10 ft, Fly 40 ft.), a centipede (40 ft, Climb 40 ft.), or a toad (40 ft, Swim 40 ft.), or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics are the same in each form, except for its Speed. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Scare: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , one creature within 20 feet.
Failure: The target has the Frightened condition. At the end of each of its turns, the target repeats the save, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

monsters.name: Rakshasa
monsters.description:
Rakshasa

Greater Magic Resistance: The rakshasa automatically succeeds on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, and the attack rolls of spells automatically miss it. Without the rakshasa's permission, no spell can observe the rakshasa remotely or detect its thoughts, creature type, or alignment.

Fiendish Restoration: If the rakshasa dies outside Gehenna, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly and revives with all its Hit Points in Gehenna.

Multiattack: The rakshasa makes three Cursed Touch attacks.

Action — Cursed Touch (2d6 slashing): If the target is a creature, it is cursed. While cursed, the target gains no benefit from finishing a Short or Long Rest.

Baleful Command: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , each enemy in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the rakshasa.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Frightened and Incapacitated conditions until the start of the rakshasa's next turn.

Spellcasting: The rakshasa casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Disguise Self, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion
1/Day Each: Fly, Invisibility, Major Image, Plane Shift

monsters.name: Sahuagin Warrior
monsters.description:
Sahuagin
Sahuagin Warrior

Blood Frenzy: The sahuagin warrior has Advantage on attack rolls against any creature that doesn't have all its Hit Points.

Limited Amphibiousness: The sahuagin warrior can breathe air and water, but it must be submerged at least once every 4 hours to avoid suffocating outside water.

Shark Telepathy: The sahuagin warrior can magically control sharks within 120 feet of itself, using a special telepathy.

Multiattack: The sahuagin warrior makes two Claw attacks.

Action — Claw (1d6 slashing)

Aquatic Charge: The sahuagin warrior swims up to its Swim Speed straight toward an enemy it can see.

monsters.name: Spirit Naga
monsters.description:
Spirit Naga

Fiendish Restoration: If it dies, the spirit naga returns to life in 1d6 days and regains all its Hit Points. Only a Wish spell can prevent this trait from functioning.

Multiattack: The spirit naga makes three attacks, using Bite or Necrotic Ray in any combination.

Action — Bite (1d6 piercing)

Action — Necrotic Ray (6d6 necrotic)

Spellcasting: The spirit naga casts one of the following spells, requiring no Somatic or Material components and using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Mage Hand, Minor Illusion, Water Breathing
2/Day Each: Detect Thoughts, Dimension Door, Hold Person (level 3), Lightning Bolt (level 4)

monsters.name: Succubus
monsters.description:
Succubus

Incubus Form: When the succubus finishes a Long Rest, it can shape-shift into an Incubus, using that stat block instead of this one.

Multiattack: The succubus makes one Fiendish Touch attack and uses Charm or Draining Kiss.

Action — Fiendish Touch (2d10 psychic)

Charm: The succubus casts Dominate Person (level 8 version), requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ).

Draining Kiss: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , one creature Charmed by the succubus within 5 feet.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.
Failure or Success: The target's Hit Point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.

Shape-Shift: The succubus shape-shifts into a Medium or Small Humanoid, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics are the same in each form, except its Fly Speed is available only in its true form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monsters.name: Vrock
monsters.description:
Vrock

Demonic Restoration: If the vrock dies outside the Abyss, its body dissolves into ichor, and it gains a new body instantly, reviving with all its Hit Points somewhere in the Abyss.

Magic Resistance: The vrock has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The vrock makes two Shred attacks.

Action — Shred (2d6 piercing)

Spores: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the vrock.
Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. While Poisoned, the target takes at the start of each of its turns. Emptying a flask of Holy Water on the target ends the effect early.

Stunning Screech: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 20-foot Emanation originating from the vrock (demons succeed automatically).
Failure: damage, and the target has the Stunned condition until the end of the vrock's next turn.

monsters.name: Cloud Giant
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The cloud giant makes two attacks, using Thunderous Mace or Thundercloud in any combination. It can replace one attack with a use of Spellcasting to cast .

Action — Thunderous Mace (3d8 bludgeoning)

Action — Thundercloud (3d6 thunder): and the target has the Incapacitated condition until the end of its next turn.

Spellcasting: The cloud giant casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Fog Cloud, Light
1/Day Each: Control Weather, Gaseous Form, Telekinesis

Misty Step: The cloud giant casts Misty Step, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

monsters.name: Ettin
monsters.description:
Action — Battleaxe (2d8 slashing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Action — Morningstar (2d8 piercing): and the target has Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

Multiattack: The ettin makes one Battleaxe attack and one Morningstar attack.

monsters.name: Fire Giant
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The fire giant makes two attacks, using Flame Sword or Hammer Throwin any combination.

Action — Flame Sword (4d6 slashing)

Action — Hammer Throw (3d10 bludgeoning): and the target is pushed up to 15 feet straight away from the fire giant and has Disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn.

monsters.name: Frost Giant
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The frost giant makes two attacks, using Frost Axe or Great Bow in any combination.

Action — Frost Axe (2d12)

Action — Great Bow (2d10 piercing): and the target's Speed decreases by 10 feet until the end of its next turn.

War Cry: The frost giant or one creature of its choice that can see or hear it gains and has Advantage on attack rolls until the start of the frost giant's next turn.

monsters.name: Hill Giant
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The hill giant makes two attacks, using Tree Club or Trash Lob in any combination.

Action — Tree Club (3d8 bludgeoning): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Action — Trash Lob (2d10 bludgeoning): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn.

Ogre

Action — Greatclub (2d8 bludgeoning)

Action — Javelin (2d6 piercing)

Action — Stone Club (3d10 bludgeoning)

Action — Boulder (2d8 bludgeoning): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Deflect Missile: Trigger: The stone giant is hit by a ranged attack roll and takes Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage from it.
Response: The stone giant reduces the damage it takes from the attack by , and if that damage is reduced to 0, the stone giant can redirect some of the attack's force.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one creature the stone giant can see within 60 feet.
Failure: damage.

monsters.name: Storm Giant
monsters.description:
Amphibious: The storm giant can breathe air and water.

Multiattack: The storm giant makes two attacks, using Storm Sword or Thunderbolt in any combination.

Action — Storm Sword (4d6 slashing)

Action — Thunderbolt (2d12 lightning): and the target has the Blinded and Deafened conditions until the start of the storm giant's next turn.

Lightning Storm: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 10-foot-radius, 40-feet-high Cylinder originating from a point the storm giant can see within 500 feet.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

Spellcasting: The storm giant casts one of the following spells, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Light
1/Day: Control Weather
monsters.speed: 50 ft, fly 25 ft, swim 50 ft, hover

monsters.name: Troll Limb
monsters.description:
Regeneration: The troll limb regains Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Troll Spawn: The limb uncannily has the same senses as a whole troll limb. If the limb isn't destroyed within 24 hours, roll 1d12#On a 12{roll 1d12}. On a 12, the limb turns into a Troll. Otherwise, the limb withers away.

Action — Rend (2d4 slashing)

monsters.name: Troll
monsters.description:
Loathsome Limbs: If the troll ends any turn Bloodied and took 15+ Slashing damage during that turn, one of the trolll's limbs is severed, falls into the troll's space, and becomes a Troll Limb. The limb acts immediately after the troll's turn. The troll has 1 Exhaustion level for each missing limb, and it grows replacement limbs the next time it regains Hit Points.

Regeneration: The troll regains Hit Points at the start of each of its turns if it has at least 1 Hit Point.

Multiattack: The troll makes three Rend attacks.

Action — Rend (2d6 slashing)

Charge: The troll moves up to half its Speed straight toward an enemy it can see.

monsters.name: Archmage
monsters.description:
Mages
Token artwork by Forgotten Adventures.

Magic Resistance: The archmage has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Multiattack: The archmage makes four Arcane Burst attacks.

Action — Arcane Burst (4d10 force)

Misty Step: The archmage casts Misty Step, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

Protective Magic: The archmage casts Counterspell or Shield in response to the spell's trigger, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

Spellcasting: The archmage casts one of the following spells, using Intelligence as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ):
At Will: Detect Magic, Detect Thoughts, Disguise Self, Invisibility, Light, Mage Armor (included in AC), Mage Hand, Prestidigitation
2/Day Each: Fly, Lightning Bolt (level 7 version)
1/Day Each: Cone of Cold (level 9 version), Mind Blank (cast before combat), Scrying, Teleport

monsters.name: Assassin
monsters.description:
Action — Shortsword (1d6 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the assassin's next turn.

Action — Light Crossbow (1d8 piercing)

Evasion: If the assassin is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, the assassin instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the save and only half damage if it fails. It can't use this trait if it has the Incapacitated condition.

Multiattack: The assassin makes three attacks, using Shortsword or Light Crossbow in any combination.

Cunning Action: The assassin takes the Dash, the Disengage, or Hide action.

Bandits

Action — Pistol (1d10 piercing)

Bloodied Frenzy: While Bloodied, the berserker has Advantage on attack rolls and saving throws.

Training: The commoner has proficiency in one skill of the DM's choice and has Advantage whenever it makes an ability check using that skill.

Cultist

Action — Ritual Sickle (1d4)

monsters.description:
Action — Spear (2d6 piercing)

Shield Bash: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one creature within 5 ft. that the gladiator can see.
Failure: damage. If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Parry: Trigger: The gladiator is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The gladiator adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

Guard Captain

Knight

Action — Greatsword (2d6 slashing)

Mage

monsters.name: Pirate Captain
monsters.description:
Pirates
Pirate Captain

Action — Rapier (2d8 piercing): and the pirate captain has Advantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of this turn.

Action — Pistol (2d10 piercing): Ranged Attack Roll: +7, range 30/90 ft. Hit: 15 (2d10 + 4) Piercing damage.

Multiattack: The pirate captain makes three attacks, using Rapier or Pistol in any combination.

Captain's Charm: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , one creature the pirate captain can see within 30 feet.
Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the start of the pirate captain's next turn.

Riposte: Trigger: The pirate captain is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon.
Response: The pirate captain adds 3 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss. On a miss, the pirate captain makes one Rapier attack against the triggering creature if within range.

monsters.name: Pirate
monsters.description:
Pirates
Pirate

Action — Dagger (1d4 piercing)

Multiattack: The pirate makes two Dagger attacks. It can replace one attack with a use of Enthralling Panache.

Enthralling Panache: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , the pirate can see within 30 feet.
Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the start of the pirate's next turn.

monsters.name: Priest Acolyte
monsters.description:
Action — Mace (1d6 bludgeoning)

Action — Radiant Flame (2d6 radiant)

At Will: Light, Thaumaturgy

Divine Aid: The priest acolyte casts Bless, Dispel Magic, Healing Word, or Lesser Restoration, using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting.

monsters.name: Priest
monsters.description:
Action — Mace (1d6 bludgeoning)

Cunning Action: The spy takes the Dash, the Disengage, or Hide action.

monsters.name: Tough Boss
monsters.description:
Toughs
Tough Boss

Action — Warhammer (2d8 bludgeoning): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the tough boss pushes the target up to 10 feet straight away from itself.

Action — Heavy Crossbow (2d10 piercing)

Pack Tactics: The tough boss has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the tough boss's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Multiattack: The tough boss makes two attacks, using Warhammer or Heavy Crossbow in any combination.

monsters.name: Tough
monsters.description:
Toughs
Tough

Action — Mace (1d6 bludgeoning)

Action — Heavy Crossbow (1d10 piercing)

monsters.name: Vampire Familiar
monsters.description:
Vampires
Vampire Familiar

Vampiric Connection: While the vampire familiar and its vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with the vampire familiar telepathically, and the vampire can perceive through the vampire familiar's senses.

Multiattack: The vampire familiar makes two Umbral Dagger attacks.

Action — Umbral Dagger (1d4): If the target is reduced to 0 Hit Points by this attack, the target becomes Stable but has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. While it has the Poisoned condition, the target has the Paralyzed condition.

Deathless Agility: The vampire familiar takes the Dash or Disengage action.

monsters.name: Warrior Infantry
monsters.description:
Warriors
Warrior Infantry

Action — Spear (1d6 piercing)

Pack Tactics: The warrior infantry has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the warrior infantry's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

monsters.name: Warrior Veteran
monsters.description:
Warriors

Action — Greatsword (2d6 slashing)

Action — Heavy Crossbow (2d10 piercing)

Multiattack: The warrior veteran makes two Greatsword or Heavy Crossbow attacks.

Parry: Trigger: The warrior veteran is hit by a melee attack roll while holding a weapon. Response: The warrior veteran adds 2 to its AC against that attack, possibly causing it to miss.

monsters.name: Avatar of Death
monsters.description:
Skull (Deck of Many Things)

Incorporeal Movement: The avatar can move through other creatures and objects as if they were DifficultTerrain. It takes 5 (1d10) Force damage if it ends its turn inside an object.

Multiattack: The avatar makes a number of Reaping Scythe attacks equal to half the summoner's Proficiency Bonus (rounded up).
Number of Attacks

Reaping Scythe: Melee Attack Roll: Automatic hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 ( 1d8 + 3 ) Slashing damage plus 4 (1d8) Necrotic damage.
monsters.languages: All languages known to its summoner

monsters.description: Action — Hooves (1d8 bludgeoning): Melee Attack Roll: +5, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d8 + 3) Bludgeoning damage.

monsters.name: Ankheg
monsters.description:
Tunneler: The ankheg can burrow through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

Action — Bite (2d6 piercing): Melee Attack Roll: (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the ankheg), reach 5 ft. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ).

Acid Spray: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 30-ft-long, 5-ft-wide .
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

monsters.name: Axe Beak
monsters.description: Action — Beak (1d8 piercing)

monsters.name: Basilisk
monsters.description:
Action — Bite (2d6 piercing)

Petrifying Gaze: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 30-foot . If the basilisk sees its reflection in the , the basilisk must make this save.
First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.

monsters.name: Behir
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The behir makes one Bite attack and uses Constrict.

Action — Bite (2d12 piercing)

Constrict: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature the behir can see within 5 ft.
Failure: damage. The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC ), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Swallow: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the behir (the behir can have only one creature swallowed at a time).
Failure: The behir swallows the target, which is no longer Grappled. While swallowed, a creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the behir, and takes damage at the start of each of the behir's turns.
If the behir takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from the swallowed creature, the behir must succeed on a at the end of that turn or regurgitate the creature, which falls in a space within 10 ft. of the behir and has the Prone condition. If the behir dies, a swallowed creature is no longer Restrained and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.

Lightning Breath: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 90-foot-long, 5-foot-wide .
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

monsters.name: Bulette
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The bulette makes two Bite attacks.

Action — Bite (2d12 piercing)

Deadly Leap: The bulette spends 5 feet of movement to jump to a space within 15 feet that contains one or more Large or smaller creatures.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in the bulette's destination space.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Prone condition.
Success: Half damage, and the target is pushed 5 feet straight away from the bulette.

Leap: The bulette jumps up to 30 feet by spending 10 feet of movement.

monsters.name: Chimera
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The chimera makes one Ram attack, one Bite attack, and one Claw attack. It can replace the Claw attack with a use of Fire Breath if available.

Action — Bite (2d6 piercing): or damage if the chimera had Advantage on the attack roll.

Action — Claw (1d6 slashing)

Action — Ram (1d12 bludgeoning): If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Fire Breath: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Cone.
Failure: damage.
Success:Half damage.

monsters.name: Cockatrice
monsters.description:
Action — Petrifying Bite (1d4 piercing): If the target is a creature, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.

monsters.name: Death Dog
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The death dog makes two Bite attacks.

Action — Bite (1d4 piercing): If the target is a creature, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
First Failure: The target has the Poisoned condition. While Poisoned, the target's Hit Point maximum doesn't return to normal when finishing a Long Rest, and it repeats the save every 24 hours that elapse, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Subsequent Failures: The Poisoned target's Hit Point maximum decreases by 5 (1d10).

monsters.name: Doppelganger
monsters.description:
Doppelganger

Multiattack: The doppelganger makes two Slam attacks and uses Unsettling Visage if available.

Action — Slam (2d6 bludgeoning): (with Advantage during the first round of each combat), reach 5 ft. .

Read Thoughts: The doppelganger casts Detect Thoughts, requiring no spell components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ).

Unsettling Visage: Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Emanation originating from the doppelganger that can see the doppelganger.
Failure: The target has the Frightened condition and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

Shape-Shift: The doppelganger shape-shifts into a Medium or smaller, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monsters.name: Drider
monsters.description:
Spider Climb: The drider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Sunlight Sensitivity: While in sunlight, the drider has Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Web Walker: The drider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and the drider knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

Multiattack: The drider makes three attacks, using Foreleg or Poison Burst in any combination.

Action — Foreleg (2d8 piercing)

Action — Poison Burst (3d6 poison)

Magic of the Spider Queen: The drider casts Darkness, Faerie Fire, or Web, requiring no Material components and using Wisdom as the spellcasting ability (spell save DC ).

monsters.name: Ettercap
monsters.description:
Spider Climb: The ettercap can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Action — Bite (1d6 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the start of the ettercap's next turn.

Web Walker: The ettercap ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and the ettercap knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

Web Strand: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature the ettercap can see within 30 feet.
Failure: The target has the Restrained condition until the web is destroyed (AC 10; HP 5; Vulnerability to Fire damage; Immunity to Bludgeoning, Poison, and Psychic damage).

Multiattack: The ettercap makes one Bite attack and one Claw attack.

Action — Claw (2d4 slashing)

Reel: The ettercap pulls within 30 feet of itself that is Restrained by its Web Strand up to 25 feet straight toward itself.

monsters.name: Flying Snake
monsters.description:
Flyby: The flying snake doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

Action — Bite (2d4 piercing)

monsters.name: Giant Vulture
monsters.description:
Pack Tactics: The giant vulture has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the giant vulture's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Action — Gouge (2d6 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of its next turn.

monsters.name: Griffon
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The griffon makes two Rend attacks.

Action — Rend (1d8 piercing): If the target is a Medium or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from both of the griffon's front claws.

monsters.name: Harpy
monsters.description:
Action — Claw (2d4 slashing)

Luring Song: The harpy sings a magical melody, which lasts until the harpy's Concentration ends on it.
Wisdom Saving Throw: DC , each Humanoid and Giant in a 300-foot Emanation originating from the harpy when the song starts.
Failure: The target has the Charmed condition until the song ends and repeats the save at the end of each of its turns. While Charmed, the target has the Incapacitated condition and ignores the Luring Song of other harpies.
If the target is more than 5 feet from the harpy, the target moves on its turn toward the harpy by the most direct route, trying to get within 5 feet of the harpy. It doesn't avoid OpportunityAttacks; however, before moving into damaging terrain (such as lava or a pit) and whenever it takes damage from a source other than the harpy, the target repeats the save.
Success: The target is immune to this harpy's Luring Song for 24 hours.

monsters.name: Hippogriff
monsters.description:
Flyby: The hippogriff doesn't provoke an Opportunity Attack when it flies out of an enemy's reach.

Multiattack: The hippogriff makes two Rend attacks.

Action — Rend (1d8 slashing)

monsters.name: Hydra
monsters.description:
Hold Breath: The hydra can hold its breath for 1 hour.

Multiple Heads: The hydra has five heads. Whenever the hydra takes 25 damage or more on a single turn, one of its heads dies. The hydra dies if all its heads are dead. At the end of each of its turns when it has at least one living head, the hydra grows two heads for each of its heads that died since its last turn, unless it has taken Fire damage since its last turn. The hydra regains 20 Hit Points when it grows new heads.

Reactive Heads: For each head the hydra has beyond one, it gets an extra Reaction that can be used only for OpportunityAttacks.

Multiattack: The hydra makes as many Bite attacks as it has heads.

Action — Bite (1d10 piercing)

monsters.name: Kraken
monsters.description:
Amphibious: The kraken can breathe air and water.

Legendary Resistance: If the kraken fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Siege Monster: The kraken deals double damage to objects and structures.

Multiattack: The kraken makes two Tentacle attacks and uses Fling, Lightning Strike, or Swallow.

Action — Tentacle (4d6 bludgeoning): The target has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from one of ten tentacles, and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Fling: The kraken throws a Large or smaller creature Grappled by it to a space it can see within 60 feet of itself that isn’t in the air.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , the creature thrown and each creature in the destination space.
Failure: damage, and the target has the Prone condition.
Success: Half damage.

Lightning Strike: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , one creature the kraken can see within 120 feet.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

Swallow: Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , Grappled by the kraken (it can have up to four creatures swallowed at a time).
Failure: damage. If the target is Large or smaller, it is swallowed and no longer Grappled. A swallowed creature has the Restrained condition, has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the kraken, and takes damage at the start of each of its turns.
If the kraken takes 50 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the kraken must succeed on a at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 10 feet of the kraken with the Prone condition. If the kraken dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained condition and can escape from the corpse using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.

Storm Bolt: The kraken uses Lightning Strike.

Toxic Ink: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Emanation originating from the kraken while it is underwater.
Failure: The target has the Blinded and Poisoned conditions until the end of the kraken's next turn. The kraken then moves up to its Speed.
Failure or Success: The kraken can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.
monsters.languages: Understands Abyssal, Celestial, Infernal, and Primordial but can’t speak

monsters.name: Manticore
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The manticore makes three attacks, using Rend or Tail Spike in any combination.

Action — Rend (1d8 slashing)

Action — Tail Spike (1d8 piercing)

monsters.name: Medusa
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The medusa makes two Claw attacks and one Snake Hair attack, or it makes three Poison Ray attacks.

Action — Claw (2d6 slashing)

Action — Snake Hair (1d4 piercing)

Action — Poison Ray (2d8 poison)

Petrifying Gaze: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 30-foot . If the medusa sees its reflection in the , the medusa must make this save.
First Failure: The target has the Restrained condition and repeats the save at the end of its next turn if it is still Restrained, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Second Failure: The target has the Petrified condition instead of the Restrained condition.

monsters.name: Merrow
monsters.description:
Amphibious: The merrow can breathe air and water.

Multiattack: The merrow makes two attacks, using Bite, Claw, or Harpoon in any combination.

Action — Bite (1d4 piercing): and the target has the Poisoned condition until the end of the merrow's next turn.

Action — Claw (2d4 slashing)

Action — Harpoon (2d6 piercing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, the merrow pulls the target up to 15 feet straight toward itself.

monsters.name: Mimic
monsters.description:
Mimic

Adhesive (Object Form Only): The mimic adheres to anything that touches it. A Huge or smaller creature adhered to the mimic has the Grappled condition (escape DC ). Ability checks made to escape this grapple have Disadvantage.

Action — Bite (1d8 piercing): Melee Attack Roll: (with Advantage if the target is Grappled by the mimic), reach 5 ft. Hit: damage—or damage if the target is Grappled by the mimic.

Action — Pseudopod (1d8 bludgeoning): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ). Ability checks made to escape this grapple have Disadvantage.

Shape-Shift: The mimic shape-shifts to resemble a Medium or Small object while retaining its game statistics, or it returns to its true blob form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

monsters.name: Minotaur of Baphomet
monsters.description:
Minotaur of Baphomet

Action — Abyssal Glaive (1d12 slashing)

Action — Gore (4d6 piercing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature and the minotaur of baphomet moved 10+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 3d6 piercing damage and has the Prone condition.

monsters.name: Owlbear
monsters.description:
Owlbears
Owlbear

Multiattack: The owlbear makes two Rend attacks.

Action — Rend (2d8 slashing)

monsters.name: Phase Spider
monsters.description:
Ethereal Sight: The phase spider can see 60 feet into the Ethereal Plane when it is on the Material Plane.

Spider Climb: The phase spider can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Web Walker: The phase spider ignores movement restrictions caused by webs, and the phase spider knows the location of any other creature in contact with the same web.

Multiattack: The phase spider makes two Bite attacks.

Action — Bite (1d10 piercing): If this damage reduces the target to 0 Hit Points, the target becomes Stable, and it has the Poisoned condition for 1 hour. While Poisoned, the target also has the Paralyzed condition.

Ethereal Jaunt: The phase spider teleports from the Material Plane to the Ethereal Plane or vice versa.

monsters.name: Purple Worm
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The purple worm makes one Bite attack and one Tail Stinger attack.

Tunneler: The purple worm can burrow through solid rock at half its Burrow Speed and leaves a 10-foot-diameter tunnel in its wake.

Action — Bite (3d8 piercing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Action — Tail Stinger (2d6)

Swallow: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the purple worm (it can have up to three creatures swallowed at a time).
Failure: The target is swallowed by the purple worm, and the Grappled conditions end. A swallowed creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the purple worm, and takes damage at the start of each of the purple worm's turns.
If the purple worm takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the purple worm must succeed on a at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the purple worm and has the Prone condition. If the purple worm dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained and can escape from the corpse by using 20 feet of movement, exiting Prone.

monsters.name: Remorhaz
monsters.description:
Heat Aura: At the end of each of the remorhaz's turns, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the remorhaz takes damage.

Action — Bite (2d10 piercing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ), and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Swallow: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the remorhaz (it can have up to two creatures swallowed at a time).
Failure: The target is swallowed by the remorhaz, and the Grappled condition ends. A swallowed creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions, it has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the remorhaz, and it takes damage at the start of each of the remorhaz's turns.
If the remorhaz takes 30 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the remorhaz must succeed on a at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 5 feet of the remorhaz and has the Prone condition. If the remorhaz dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained condition and can escape from the corpse by using 15 feet of movement, exiting Prone.

monsters.name: Roc
monsters.description:
Multiattack: The roc makes two Beak attacks. It can replace one attack with a Talons attack.

Action — Beak (3d12 piercing)

Action — Talons (4d6 slashing): If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Grappled condition (escape DC ) from both talons, and it has the Restrained condition until the grapple ends.

Swoop: If the roc has a creature Grappled, the roc flies up to half its Fly Speed without provoking OpportunityAttacks and drops that creature.

monsters.name: Rust Monster
monsters.description:
Iron Scent: The rust monster can pinpoint the location of ferrous metal within 30 feet of itself.

Multiattack: The rust monster makes one Bite attack and uses Antennae twice.

Action — Bite (1d8 piercing)

Antennae: The rust monster targets one nonmagical metal object—armor or a weapon—worn or carried by a creature within 5 feet of itself.
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , the creature with the object.
Failure: The object takes a -1 penalty to the AC it offers (armor) or to its attack rolls (weapon). Armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10, and a weapon is destroyed if its penalty reaches -5. The penalty can be removed by casting the spell on the armor or weapon.

Destroy Metal: The rust monster touches a nonmagical metal object within 5 feet of itself that isn’t being worn or carried. The touch destroys a 1-ft of the object.

Reflexive Antennae: Trigger: An attack roll hits the rust monster. Response: The rust monster uses Antennae.

monsters.name: Stirge
monsters.description:
Stirges
Stirge

Action — Proboscis (1d6 piercing): If the target is a creature, and the stirge attaches to the target. While attached, the stirge can't make Proboscis attacks, and the target takes damage at the start of each of the stirge's turns.
The stirge can detach itself by spending 5 feet of its movement. The target or a creature within 5 feet of it can detach the stirge as an action.

monsters.name: Tarrasque
monsters.description:
Legendary Resistance: If the tarrasque fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

Magic Resistance: The tarrasque has Advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

Reflective Carapace: If the tarrasque is targeted by a Magic Missile spell or a spell that requires a ranged attack roll, roll 1d6.
On a 1–5, the tarrasque is unaffected.
On a 6, the tarrasque is unaffected and reflects the spell, turning the caster into the target.

Multiattack: The tarrasque makes one Bite attack and three other attacks, using Claw or Tail in any combination.

Action — Bite (4d12 piercing): and the target has the Grappled condition (escape DC ). Until the grapple ends, the target has the Restrained condition and can't teleport.

Action — Claw (4d8 slashing)

Action — Tail (3d8 bludgeoning): If the target is a Huge or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Thunderous Bellow: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature and each object that isn’t being worn or carried in a 150-ft. Failure: damage, and the target has the Deafened and Frightened conditions until the end of its next turn.
Success: Half damage only.

Swallow: Strength Saving Throw: DC , one Large or smaller creature Grappled by the tarrasque (it can have up to six creatures swallowed at a time).
Failure: The target is swallowed, and the Grappled condition ends. A swallowed creature has the Blinded and Restrained conditions and can't teleport, it has Total Cover against attacks and other effects outside the tarrasque, and takes damage at the start of each of the tarrasque's turns.
If the tarrasque takes 60 damage or more on a single turn from a creature inside it, the tarrasque must succeed on a at the end of that turn or regurgitate all swallowed creatures, each of which falls in a space within 10 feet of the tarrasque and has the Prone condition. If the tarrasque dies, any swallowed creature no longer has the Restrained condition and can escape from the corpse by using 20 feet of movement, exiting Prone.

Onslaught: The tarrasque moves up to half its Speed, and it makes one Claw or Tail attack.

World-Shaking Movement: The tarrasque moves up to its Speed. At the end of this movement, the tarrasque creates an instantaneous shock wave in a 60-ft. Emanation originating from itself. Creatures in that area lose Concentration and, if Medium or smaller, have the Prone condition. The tarrasque can't take this action again until the start of its next turn.

Siege Monster: The tarrasque deals double damage to objects and structures.

monsters.name: Werebear
monsters.description:
Action — Handaxe (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) (3d6 slashing)

Multiattack: The werebear makes two attacks, using Handaxe (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) or Rend (Bear or Hybrid Form Only) in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite (Bear or Hybrid Form Only) attack.

Action — Bite (Bear or Hybrid Form Only) (2d12 piercing): If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Werebear under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points.
Success: The target is immune to this werebear's' curse for 24 hours.

Action — Rend (Bear or Hybrid Form Only) (2d8 slashing)

Shape-Shift: The werebear shape-shifts into a Large bear-humanoid hybrid form or a Large bear, or it returns to its true humanoid form, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
monsters.languages: Common, can’t speak in bear form

monsters.name: Wereboar
monsters.description:
Action — Javelin (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) (3d6 piercing)

Multiattack: The wereboar makes two attacks, using Javelin (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) or Tusk (Boar or Hybrid Form Only) in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Gore (Boar or Hybrid Form Only) attack.

Action — Gore  (Boar or Hybrid Form Only) (2d8 piercing): If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Wereboar under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points.
Success: The target is immune to this wereboar's curse for 24 hours.

Action — Tusk (Boar or Hybrid Form Only) (2d6 piercing): If the target is a Medium or smaller creature and the wereboar moved 20+ feet straight toward it immediately before the hit, the target takes an extra 7 (2d6) damage and has the Prone condition.

Shape-Shift: The wereboar shape-shifts into a Medium boar-humanoid hybrid form or a Medium boar, or it returns to its true humanoid form, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
monsters.languages: Common, Can’t speak in boar form

monsters.name: Wererat
monsters.description:
Action — Hand Crossbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) (1d6 piercing)

Multiattack: The wererat makes two attacks, using Scratch or Hand Crossbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite (Rat or Hybrid Form Only) attack.

Action — Bite (Rat or Hybrid Form Only) (2d4 piercing): If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Wererat under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points.
Success: The target is immune to this wererat's' curse for 24 hours.

Action — Scratch (1d6 slashing)

Shape-Shift: The wererat shape-shifts into a Medium rat-humanoid hybrid form or a Small rat, or it returns to its true humanoid form, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
monsters.languages: Common, Can't speak in rat form

monsters.name: Weretiger
monsters.description:
Action — Longbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) (2d8 piercing)

Multiattack: The weretiger two attacks, using Scratch or Longbow (Humanoid or Hybrid Form Only) in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite (Tiger or Hybrid Form Only) attack.

Action — Bite (Tiger or Hybrid Form Only) (2d8 piercing): If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Weretiger under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points.
Success: The target is immune to this weretiger's' curse for 24 hours.

Action — Scratch (2d6 slashing)

Shape-Shift: The weretiger shape-shifts into a Large tiger-humanoid hybrid form or a Large tiger, or it returns to its true humanoid form, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.

Prowl (Tiger or Hybrid Form Only): The weretiger moves up to its Speed without provoking OpportunityAttacks. At the end of this movement, the weretiger can take the Hide action.
monsters.languages: Common, can’t speak in tiger form

monsters.name: Werewolf
monsters.description:
Action — Longbow (2d8 piercing)

Pack Tactics: The werewolf has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the werewolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Multiattack: The werewolf makes two attacks, using Scratch or Longbow in any combination. It can replace one attack with a Bite (Wolf or Hybrid Form Only) attack.

Action — Bite (Wolf or Hybrid Form Only) (2d8 piercing): If the target is a Humanoid, it is subjected to the following effect.
Constitution Saving Throw: DC .
Failure: The target is cursed. If the cursed target drops to 0 Hit Points, it instead becomes a Werewolf under the DM's control and has 10 Hit Points.
Success: The target is immune to this werewolf's curse for 24 hours.

Action — Scratch (2d6 slashing)

Shape-Shift: The werewolf shape-shifts into a Large wolf-humanoid hybrid form or a Medium wolf, or it returns to its true humanoid form, or it returns to its true form. Its game statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed.
monsters.languages: Common, can’t speak in wolf form

monsters.name: Winter Wolf
monsters.description:
Pack Tactics: The winter wolf has Advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the winter wolf's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally doesn't have the Incapacitated condition.

Action — Bite (2d6 piercing): If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it has the Prone condition.

Cold Breath: Constitution Saving Throw: DC , each creature in a 15-foot Cone.
Failure: damage.
Success: Half damage.

monsters.name: Black Pudding
monsters.description:
Amorphous: The black pudding can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Corrosive Form: A creature that hits the black pudding with a melee attack roll takes damage. Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting the black pudding and dealing any damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative −1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage to the black pudding and coming into contact with it. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches −5. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the weapon.
In 1 minute, the black pudding can eat through 2 feet of nonmagical wood or metal.

Spider Climb: The black pudding can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

Split: Trigger: While the black pudding is Large or Medium and has 10+ Hit Points, it becomes Bloodied or is subjected to Lightning or Slashing damage. Response: The black pudding splits into two new Black Pudding. Each new black pudding is one size smaller than the original black pudding and acts on its Initiative. The original black pudding's Hit Points are divided evenly between the new black puddings (round down).

Action — Dissolving Pseudopod (4d6 acid): Nonmagical armor worn by the target takes a -1 penalty to the AC it offers. The armor is destroyed if the penalty reduces its AC to 10. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the armor.

monsters.name: Gelatinous Cube
monsters.description:
Gelatinous Cube

Ooze Cube: The gelatinous cube fills its entire space and is transparent. Other creatures can enter that space, but a creature that does so is subjected to the gelatinous cube's Engulf and has Disadvantage on the saving throw.
Creatures inside the gelatinous cube have Total Cover, and the gelatinous cube can hold one Large creature or up to four Medium or Small creatures inside itself at a time.
As an action, a creature within 5 ft. of the gelatinous cube can pull a creature or an object out of the gelatinous cube by succeeding on a DC 12 Strength (Athletics), and the puller takes damage.

Transparent: Even when the gelatinous cube is in plain sight, a creature must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom (Perception) to notice the gelatinous cube if the creature hasn't witnessed the gelatinous cube move or otherwise act.

Action — Pseudopod (3d6 acid)

Engulf: The gelatinous cube moves up to its Speed without provoking Opportunity Attacks. The gelatinous cube can move through the spaces of Large or smaller creatures if it has room inside itself to contain them (see the trait).
Dexterity Saving Throw: DC , each creature whose space the gelatinous cube enters for the first time during this move.
Failure: damage, and the target is engulfed. An engulfed target is suffocating, can't cast spells with a Verbal component, has the Restrained condition, and takes damage at the start of each of the gelatinous cube's turns. When the gelatinous cube moves, the engulfed target moves with it. An engulfed target can try to escape by taking an action to make a Strength (Athletics) check. On a successful check, the target escapes and enters the nearest unoccupied space.
Success: Half damage, and the target moves to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of the gelatinous cube's. If there is no unoccupied space, the target fails the save instead.

monsters.name: Gray Ooze
monsters.description:
Gray Oozes
Gray Ooze

Amorphous: The gray ooze can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so.

Corrosive Form: Nonmagical ammunition is destroyed immediately after hitting the gray ooze and dealing any damage. Any nonmagical weapon takes a cumulative −1 penalty to attack rolls immediately after dealing damage to the gray ooze and coming into contact with it. The weapon is destroyed if the penalty reaches −5. The penalty can be removed by casting the Mending spell on the weapon.
The gray ooze can eat through 2-inch-thick, nonmagical metal or wood in 1 round.
